summary: First-principles deep research + adversarial synthesis of a self-hosted Claude-Code-like AI stack for the home-ops Talos/Flux cluster
logs: Verifying 36 distinct finalist candidates across all layers

integration.integration_matrix:
## Chosen stack (strongest coherent build)

KEEP: llama-swap (Vulkan) · LiteLLM · Open WebUI · n8n · AnythingLLM (interim, bake-off vs OWUI-RAG) · LangFuse+ClickHouse · llama.cpp embed/rerank · MetaMCP · Wyoming whisper+piper (HA) · Velero/CNPG/RustFS backups
ADD: Goose (`ghcr.io/aaif-goose/goose`, `goose serve`) as in-cluster agent runner · Claude Code headless / Goose CLI as laptop client (cloud-passthrough quality) · Telegram→n8n delegation path · speaches (OpenAI `/v1/audio/*`) · jupyter-kernel-gateway-behind-MCP (sandboxed code-exec, Phase 1)
DROP: dedicated memory layer (mem0 deleted, OWUI mem0 env removed) · Continue.dev server · OpenHands (bench) · LibreChat/Bifrost/Lobe/Windmill-as-replacement

| From (consumer) | To (provider) | Protocol | Native or Shim |
|---|---|---|---|
| Open WebUI | owui-pipelines :9099/v1 | OpenAI-compat HTTP | Native (deployed; `OPENAI_API_BASE_URL`) |
| owui-pipelines | LiteLLM :4000/v1 | OpenAI-compat HTTP | Native (router forwards to gateway) |
| n8n (AI Agent / HTTP / OpenAI nodes) | LiteLLM :4000/v1 | OpenAI-compat HTTP + virtual key | Native (creds wired manually in UI) |
| AnythingLLM | LiteLLM :4000/v1 (LLM + embed) | OpenAI-compat HTTP (`generic-openai`) | Native |
| AnythingLLM | LiteLLM rerank route → llama-swap `rerank-bge` | Cohere/Jina-style `/rerank` (NOT `/v1` chat) | Shim-ish: rerank is off-spec; verify AnythingLLM points at LiteLLM `rerank` route, not `/v1` |
| LiteLLM | llama-swap :8080/v1 | OpenAI-compat HTTP (alias→model-key) | Native (model_list `api_base`; alias must be llama-swap key, NOT GGUF filename) |
| LiteLLM | llama-swap :8080 `/v1/rerank` | Jina-style rerank | Native via LiteLLM `rerank` route |
| LiteLLM | Anthropic/OpenAI cloud | provider passthrough | Native but DISABLED (cloud-* aliases commented OFF, no key) — GATES agent/coding quality |
| LiteLLM | LangFuse :3000 | langfuse SDK callback | Native (`success/failure_callback:["langfuse"]`, env-injected) |
| LiteLLM | CNPG postgres18 (DB `litellm`) | Postgres (Prisma) | Native (`db.useExisting`) |
| LiteLLM | DragonflyDB DB4 | Redis proto (`REDIS_URL`) | Native (prompt cache) |
| LangFuse | CNPG pg + ClickHouse + DragonflyDB DB6 + RustFS S3 | PG / native CH / Redis / S3 | Native (4 backing stores; CH is dedicated/irreversible) |
| Goose (goosed `serve`) | LiteLLM :4000/v1 | OpenAI-compat HTTP (`GOOSE_PROVIDER=openai`, `OPENAI_HOST`) | Native (inherits LangFuse trace + spend + cloud passthrough) |
| Goose | MetaMCP SSE/HTTP | MCP (SSE `/metamcp/<ep>/sse` or Streamable HTTP) | Native (Goose is MCP-native; API-key auth bypasses OIDC) |
| Goose | RAG (AnythingLLM vector-search / OWUI retrieval) | MCP tool OR REST | Shim: wrap AnythingLLM `POST /v1/workspace/:slug/vector-search` as an MCP server in MetaMCP |
| Goose | git / GitHub | MCP github server + SOPS git creds | Shim: register github MCP in MetaMCP; mount git creds |
| Claude Code headless (laptop or in-cluster Job) | LiteLLM :4000 | Anthropic-compat `/v1/messages` (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`) | Native (LiteLLM exposes Anthropic shape; cloud passthrough is the quality lever) |
| n8n (delegation orchestrator) | Goose `serve` REST/SSE OR K8s Job spawn | REST/SSE or K8s API | Shim: n8n HTTP Request node → Goose, or n8n → K8s Job (agent-runner image) |
| Telegram | n8n Telegram Trigger | Telegram Bot API (long-poll/webhook, egress-only) | Native (built-in core node; allowlist chat ID) |
| n8n | faster-whisper (HA STT path) OR speaches `/v1/audio/transcriptions` | Wyoming TCP (HA) / OpenAI HTTP (agent) | Native to speaches; Wyoming pods NOT reachable by n8n/LiteLLM |
| MCP clients (Goose, Claude Code, n8n MCP node) | MetaMCP | MCP SSE / Streamable HTTP | Native (deployed; postgres18; Authentik split-auth) |
| Open WebUI (tools) | MetaMCP OpenAPI export (or mcpo fallback) | OpenAPI/REST (OWUI does NOT speak raw MCP) | Native if MetaMCP OpenAPI export reliable; else add mcpo shim |
| jupyter-kernel-gateway (code-exec) | (none — it's a tool) registered in MetaMCP | Jupyter kernel proto → wrapped by MCP shim | Shim: build image + MCP adapter + deny-egress NetworkPolicy |
| LiteLLM | speaches `/v1/audio/{transcriptions,speech}` | OpenAI-compat audio | Native (LiteLLM audio proxy → one keyed endpoint for n8n/phone) |
| Home Assistant Assist | wyoming-whisper :10300 / wyoming-piper :10200 | Wyoming TCP | Native (separate path; do NOT consolidate into speaches) |
| Velero / CNPG Barman / RustFS | s3.68cc.io | S3 (aws/rclone/boto3) | Native (durability layer; NOT replication) |
| All UIs/agents (ingress) | Traefik Gateway API → Authentik forwardAuth | HTTP + forwardAuth headers / API-key bypass for programmatic | Native (per-ns Component; VIP .17 LAN / .15 public via CF tunnel) |
glue_costs: LiteLLM cloud-* aliases: uncomment anthropic/openai blocks in configmap.yaml + add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY to litellm-secrets (SOPS). LOAD-BEARING: agent/coding quality is non-existent without this; today they're commented OFF. Effort: ~30min config + key procurement.

Goose deployment: bjw-s app-template HelmRelease + openebs-hostpath PVC (~/.config/goose + workspace) + HTTPRoute (traefik-internal .17) + authentik-forwardauth ExtensionRef. Override CMD to ['goose','serve','--host','0.0.0.0','--port','3284'] (NOT 'command: server' — that crash-loops), probe :3284. Pin ghcr.io/aaif-goose/goose by sha-<commit> or :main digest (no current semver image tag). Effort: ~half day incl smoke test of no-Docker-socket in-pod tool isolation.

RAG-as-MCP shim: wrap AnythingLLM POST /v1/workspace/:slug/vector-search (or OWUI /api/v1/retrieval) as an MCP server, register in a MetaMCP namespace, so Goose/Claude Code get retrieval as a tool. Effort: ~1 day (thin MCP wrapper image OR n8n-mediated).

AnythingLLM rerank verification: confirm its reranker config targets the LiteLLM `rerank` route (Cohere/Jina shape), NOT the /v1 chat path — and that bge-rerank is actually active under VECTOR_DB=pgvector (LanceDB-reranker dormancy claim unverified in running instance). Effort: ~2h verify, possible reconfig.

OWUI-RAG bake-off (if collapsing AnythingLLM): set VECTOR_DB=pgvector, RAG_OPENAI_API_BASE_URL→LiteLLM, RAG_RERANKING_ENGINE=external, RAG_EXTERNAL_RERANKER_URL→bge-via-llama-swap; EMPIRICALLY verify the external-reranker enum string + that bge returns scored chunks (the named acceptance gate). One-time re-index (embeddings non-portable). Effort: ~1-2 days incl A/B.

Telegram→n8n→agent delegation workflow: BotFather token (SOPS secret) + n8n workflow (Telegram Trigger → chat-ID allowlist gate → K8s Job spawn or Goose HTTP POST → result push-back). Voice: Telegram voice note → faster-whisper/speaches → text → same flow. Effort: ~1 day workflow authoring.

Agent-runner image (if using K8s-Job dispatch instead of resident Goose): thin amd64 Dockerfile wrapping goose/claude-code CLI + git + MCP, workspace PVC on bigboi fast NVMe, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL→litellm, git creds via SOPS. Effort: ~half day Dockerfile + RBAC for n8n→K8s Job.

speaches deployment: single CPU pod (ghcr.io/speaches-ai/speaches:0.9.0-rc.3-cpu, <2Gi), model PVC, optional LiteLLM /audio/* proxy registration. CPU-only on this AMD cluster (no Vulkan variant). Effort: ~2-3h. Escalation path (whisper.cpp-vulkan + Kokoro) deferred until voice complaint.

Sandboxed code-exec (Phase 1): BUILD jupyter-kernel-gateway image (no official image exists — FROM python:3.12-slim + pip install jupyter_kernel_gateway) + MCP shim adapter + strict non-root/read-only-rootfs/deny-egress Cilium NetworkPolicy + register in MetaMCP. Effort: ~1-2 days. Phase 2 (Isola/gVisor) BLOCKED on gVisor-RuntimeClass-on-Talos spike (system extension + machine-config + per-node apply under etcd quorum gate).
mcpo fallback (only if MetaMCP OpenAPI export to OWUI misbehaves): stateless proxy, pin git-<sha> tag (no semver image), no Renovate semver tracking. Effort: ~1h if needed.

Cleanup (negative cost): delete kubernetes/apps/ai/mem0/ + its ks.yaml line from ai/kustomization.yaml; remove dead MEMORY_PROVIDER/MEM0_API_BASE_URL/MEM0_API_KEY env (open-webui helmrelease.yaml lines 58-63); fix stale CLAUDE.md notes (MetaMCP listed disabled but is live; faster-whisper described as OpenAI-HTTP but is Wyoming-only). Effort: ~1h.

Renovate hardening (LOAD-BEARING safety): add packageRule pinning ghcr.io/mostlygeek/llama-swap to -vulkan-b suffix BEFORE bumping v179→v223 — otherwise auto-discovery surfaces rocm/cuda/musa tags that would push a non-functional ROCm image onto Talos. Also pin n8n off the 2.25.x beta channel. Effort: ~1h.
conflicts: DEAD INTEGRATION (must remove): OWUI MEMORY_PROVIDER=mem0_server / MEM0_* env (confirmed present helmrelease.yaml:58-63) is a no-op — upstream OWUI has no external-memory-provider concept. Pretends an integration that cannot exist; mem0 image is permanently 404.

RAG DUPLICATION: AnythingLLM RAG vs Open WebUI built-in RAG — both reuse pgvector + LiteLLM, so two RAG stacks run in parallel during the bake-off. Decisively, AnythingLLM's reranker is LanceDB-only → the cluster's bge `local-rerank` is DORMANT for AnythingLLM RAG, while OWUI can wire it via RAG_RERANKING_ENGINE=external. Collapse to one after the bge-wiring gate passes.

ROUTING DUPLICATION: owui-pipelines (model router at :9099) overlaps LiteLLM's own model groups/routing. OWUI→pipelines→LiteLLM is two hops doing similar fan-out logic. If OWUI is ever replaced, the pipelines routing must be recreated as LiteLLM model groups or it's lost. Not harmful today, but redundant routing surface.

AGENT-RUNTIME OVERLAP (resolved by layering, watch creep): Goose (agent runtime) vs n8n LangChain/AI-Agent nodes vs OWUI/AnythingLLM chat. n8n's in-workflow agent nodes can do light tool-calling against LiteLLM — overlaps Goose for simple 'automate findings' flows. Keep the boundary: n8n = event/orchestration plane that DISPATCHES to Goose; Goose = autonomous coding/file-edit runtime. Do NOT let the chat UI become the agent runtime (that's the LibreChat trap that imports Mongo+Meilisearch).

CODING-AGENT REDUNDANCY: Goose (in-cluster, OSS) and Claude Code headless (laptop/Job, proprietary) both target the coding-agent role. Intentional split — Goose for OSS-clean async in-cluster jobs, Claude Code for top-quality interactive — but they share the same LiteLLM client contract and could be confused. Continue.dev server is a 3rd candidate and should be REJECTED for the runner role (Hub-coupled, build-required, less battle-tested); keep Continue only as an optional IDE client if at all.

VOICE PROTOCOL SPLIT (by design, not a defect): Wyoming pods (HA) and speaches (agent /v1/audio) serve the SAME function via DIFFERENT protocols. Resist consolidating speaches into the HA path — HA's Wyoming integration is path-of-least-resistance and speaches is Wyoming-incapable. Two tiny pods is the correct outcome.

OBSERVABILITY WEIGHT: LangFuse+ClickHouse is the heaviest backing dep in ai/ (dedicated single-node ClickHouse, irreversible, only consumer = LangFuse). Conflicts with single-replica/KISS discipline IF only the trace viewer is used. Not an active conflict — gated on measuring ClickHouse span volume; if low and evals unused, migrate to Arize Phoenix (postgres18-only) to delete the CH pod. Default KEEP until measured.

MCP OpenAPI-vs-MCP impedance: OWUI consumes tools as OpenAPI, NOT raw MCP, while every agent (Goose/Claude Code/n8n) consumes raw MCP. MetaMCP must serve BOTH surfaces; if its OpenAPI export to OWUI is unreliable, mcpo becomes a required second proxy — a latent duplication risk in the tool-broker layer.
integration.data_flow:
RAG (goal 1): User query → Open WebUI (ai.68cc.io PWA) → owui-pipelines :9099 → LiteLLM :4000/v1 → (a) embed query via local-embed→llama-swap embed-nomic, (b) similarity search in pgvector on postgres18 (OWUI native RAG OR AnythingLLM workspace), (c) rerank top-k via LiteLLM rerank route→llama-swap rerank-bge (cross-encoder, always-on resident), (d) grounded completion via local-balanced→llama-swap qwen3-4b. Citations returned to OWUI. LangFuse traces every LiteLLM hop automatically. Agents reach the same store via AnythingLLM vector-search wrapped as an MCP tool in MetaMCP. NEW DATASTORE: none (reuses pgvector). GATE: verify bge external-reranker wiring before retiring AnythingLLM.

TASK AUTOMATION / findings (goal 2): Trigger (GitHub/Linear webhook, cron, or Telegram) → n8n (webhook path bypasses Authentik; editor SSO-gated) → n8n calls LiteLLM for inference and/or DISPATCHES a delegated job to Goose (HTTP Request node → goosed serve, or spawns a K8s Job running the agent-runner) → agent uses MetaMCP tools (github, RAG vector-search, filesystem, sandboxed code-exec via jupyter-kernel-gateway behind MCP) → writes findings back to RAG (AnythingLLM ingest, like existing linkwarden-to-anythingllm.json) and/or opens a PR → n8n notifies (Telegram/Discord). State on postgres18 DB n8n. NEW DATASTORE: none.

PHONE DELEGATION → in-cluster agent (goals 3+4): Phone Telegram message (or voice note) → Telegram Bot API (long-poll/webhook, egress-only — NO new public port) → n8n Telegram Trigger → chat-ID allowlist gate → [voice: faster-whisper or speaches → text] → n8n spawns K8s Job / POSTs Goose serve → runner sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://litellm:4000 (Anthropic-compat /v1/messages, verified) OR OPENAI_HOST=http://litellm:4000/v1 → LiteLLM routes per virtual-key allow-list to local llama-swap OR limited cloud passthrough (Anthropic/OpenAI) → runner works in workspace PVC (bigboi fast NVMe), commits/PRs via SOPS git creds → LangFuse traces the run → n8n Telegram action pushes PR link/result back to phone. Mobile chat (sub-problem #1) rides the unchanged OWUI PWA via Cloudflare tunnel → Traefik .15 → Authentik. NEW DATASTORE: none (Telegram is external SaaS; workspace is a PVC).

LOCAL INFERENCE (goal 5): Any client (OWUI/n8n/AnythingLLM/Goose/Claude-Code) → LiteLLM :4000/v1 (alias e.g. local-balanced/local-coder/local-embed/local-rerank) → llama-swap :8080/v1 on bigboi Navi 21 dGPU (Vulkan, ROCm dead) → hot-swap: chat models (qwen3-14b/coder) swap exclusively into the 16GB budget via `chat` group (swap:true exclusive:true) while embed-nomic+rerank-bge stay resident via `always-on` group (swap:false persistent:true). Completion returns up the same chain. NEW DATASTORE: none (GGUFs on openebs-hostpath-fast PVC; init-container HF prefetch).

CLOUD PASSTHROUGH (goal 5, the quality lever): Anthropic-SDK client (Claude Code headless, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=litellm) or OpenAI-SDK client → LiteLLM /v1/messages or /v1/chat → LiteLLM checks the virtual key's model allow-list → if alias=cloud-sonnet/cloud-opus AND key permits → passthrough to real Anthropic/OpenAI with the API key in litellm-secrets, spend-capped + LangFuse-traced; if alias=local-* or key forbids cloud → routed to llama-swap instead. This is the SAME chokepoint that enforces kid-safe (block cloud-*, allow local-balanced/local-fast). BLOCKER TODAY: cloud-* aliases are commented OFF (confirmed configmap.yaml:83-93) and no key is set — agent/coding quality is unusable until uncommented + keyed. NEW DATASTORE: none.
new_datastores_required: NONE for the recommended primary stack — every chosen component reuses CNPG postgres18 (+pgvector), DragonflyDB, ClickHouse (LangFuse-only, already running), Dolt, or RustFS S3. This is the deliberate single-datastore-discipline win.

CONDITIONAL (rejected by default): Redis Agent Memory Server would force a NEW redis-stack-server pod (RediSearch/vector; DragonflyDB cannot back it) — only if a future in-cluster agent genuinely needs shared cross-run memory, adopted via its MCP interface as a deliberate exception. Not part of the recommended build.

CONDITIONAL (Phase 2, blocked): Isola sandboxed-exec needs a gVisor RuntimeClass on Talos (not a datastore, but a hard infra dep requiring system-extension + machine-config). Phase 1 jupyter-kernel-gateway adds zero datastore.

AVOIDED by rejection: LibreChat (Mongo+Meilisearch+rag-api+pgvector — 2-4 new stateful pods), Bifrost default SQLite (parallel state outside CNPG backup), self-built mem0 (no image), Onyx/RAGFlow/Verba (new heavyweight index + MinIO/Redis).

strongest_objections: THE CLOUD-PASSTHROUGH BLOCKER INVALIDATES HALF THE STACK'S PRIORITIES. Verified in litellm/app/configmap.yaml: cloud-haiku/cloud-sonnet aliases are commented OFF (lines 81-89) and no Anthropic key is set. The proposal itself admits 'agent/coding quality is unusable until uncommented + keyed' and that 'there is no local-model substitute' for the coding agent. So the ENTIRE Claude-Code-like goal (goals 2+4) is gated on one 30-minute config change + an API key — NOT on adding Goose, jupyter-kernel-gateway, speaches, a Telegram bridge, or an MCP sandbox layer. The stack proposes ~6 new components to chase a goal that is currently blocked by a comment character. That is textbook building-the-architecture-before-fixing-the-actual-constraint. Recommendation: uncomment cloud aliases + add key FIRST, then run Claude Code / Goose as a LAPTOP CLI against the existing LiteLLM (which the proposal itself concedes is 'the cheapest path... that satisfies reqs 1-5 with the least new surface'). Measure whether that hits all 5 goals before deploying ANY in-cluster agent runner.

THE SANDBOXED-CODE-EXEC LAYER (jupyter-kernel-gateway + MCP shim + NetworkPolicy, ~1-2 days, OR Isola blocked on a gVisor-on-Talos spike) IS PURE INFRA-FOR-A-DIAGRAM. The north star is single-operator delegation of coding tasks. A single trusted operator's own coding agent does NOT need kernel-level isolation of untrusted code — the agent IS you. The proposal's own Goose rec states 'the agent runs tools inside its own pod, which IS the isolation boundary on K8s.' If the pod boundary is the isolation story for Goose, it is also the isolation story for code-exec. Standing up gVisor on an immutable-rootfs Talos cluster (system extension + machine-config + per-node apply under the etcd-quorum gate) to sandbox code YOU wrote and YOU delegated is effort spent on a threat model that doesn't exist for this user. CUT the entire MCP-sandbox sub-layer until/unless kids or untrusted callers actually use it.

MetaMCP IS AN AGGREGATION LAYER WITH ALMOST NOTHING TO AGGREGATE. Confirmed live in services/kustomization.yaml (line `- ./metamcp/ks.yaml`, uncommented — CLAUDE.md's 'disabled' note is stale). MetaMCP's whole value is collapsing MANY MCP servers behind one endpoint with namespacing. The proposed stack has essentially one real consumer (a not-yet-deployed Goose) and a github MCP server. The proposal even defers ToolHive saying it 'solves a sprawl problem you don't have yet' — that exact logic indicts MetaMCP too. For one operator with 1-2 MCP servers, Goose/Claude Code can point at those servers directly; a Postgres-backed aggregation gateway is premature generality. It is already deployed so the cost is sunk, but do NOT add mcpo on top of it, and do NOT treat MetaMCP as load-bearing for the goal.

LANGFUSE + DEDICATED IRREVERSIBLE CLICKHOUSE IS THE HEAVIEST DEPENDENCY IN THE STACK FOR A SINGLE-OPERATOR HOMELAB. Confirmed: open-webui references langfuse, and the standalone ClickHouse exists ONLY to serve Langfuse with no other consumer. The proposal correctly flags this as 'over-engineered IF you are only using the trace viewer and cost dashboard — that workload fits comfortably in Postgres' and that Arize Phoenix (postgres18-only, no ClickHouse) would let you delete the CH pod. For a single user debugging their own agent, a Postgres-backed trace viewer is sufficient; a column-store analytics DB is for teams running evals at volume. The 'keep until measured' posture is defensible since CH is sunk cost, but the HONEST YAGNI verdict is: this layer was sized for an org, not one person, and Phoenix is the right-sized replacement.

owui-pipelines IS A REDUNDANT ROUTING HOP. The proposal's own conflicts section names it: 'owui-pipelines (model router at :9099) overlaps LiteLLM's own model groups/routing. OWUI->pipelines->LiteLLM is two hops doing similar fan-out logic.' LiteLLM already does aliasing, fallback, and routing. A second router in front of it whose job is keyword->model selection is logic that belongs in LiteLLM model groups. This is a layer that exists because it was easy to add, not because the goal requires two routing planes.
what_to_cut: DELETE mem0 entirely (kubernetes/apps/ai/mem0/ + its `- ./mem0/ks.yaml` line in ai/kustomization.yaml, confirmed present). The image is permanently 404 and the proposal's own memory-layer verdict is DROP THE LAYER. It is dead infra pretending to be a roadmap item.

REMOVE the dead OWUI MEMORY_PROVIDER/MEM0_API_BASE_URL/MEM0_API_KEY env block (open-webui helmrelease.yaml lines 57-63, confirmed). It is a confirmed no-op that fakes a working integration. Negative-cost cleanup.

CUT the sandboxed-code-exec sub-layer (jupyter-kernel-gateway-behind-MCP Phase 1 AND Isola/gVisor Phase 2). Untrusted-code isolation is not a single-operator requirement; the pod boundary already isolates the agent. This is the single largest piece of unearned infra in the proposal (~1-2 days + a blocked gVisor-on-Talos spike).

DO NOT ADD a resident in-cluster Goose `serve` deployment as the FIRST move. The proposal itself says the cheapest path is laptop-CLI Goose/Claude Code against existing LiteLLM. Defer the in-cluster pod until you have a concrete async/scheduled delegation job that the laptop CLI cannot serve.
DO NOT ADD mcpo. MetaMCP already exports OpenAPI; a second proxy is speculative redundancy.

DO NOT run OWUI built-in RAG as a parallel stack alongside AnythingLLM indefinitely — pick ONE RAG app. Two pgvector RAG consumers for one user is duplication; collapse fast or don't start the bake-off.
Schedule the LangFuse->Arize Phoenix migration to delete the dedicated ClickHouse pod once span volume is measured (proposal's own trip-wire). For one operator this is almost certainly justified.
what_to_keep: llama-swap (Vulkan) — load-bearing, the only thing that makes the AMD GPU usable; single-GPU hot-swap is irreplaceable. Bump the stale pin and add the Renovate -vulkan-b rule (the real risk: auto-discovery could push a non-functional ROCm tag).

LiteLLM — the single gateway/chokepoint; reuses postgres18+Dragonfly, enforces per-key allow-lists and cloud passthrough. This is the actual control point for goals 1-5. UNCOMMENT cloud aliases + add key — the highest-leverage single action in the whole stack.
Open WebUI — thin chat UI + PWA; covers the phone-chat half of goal 3 with zero new datastores.

n8n — the event/automation plane (goal 2) AND the natural Telegram->delegation orchestrator (goals 3+4) reusing existing postgres18. The Telegram->n8n->LiteLLM delegation path is the genuinely high-value addition because it reuses already-deployed components and opens no new public port.
ONE RAG app (AnythingLLM as-is, or collapse into OWUI — but not both long-term). RAG is goal 1; pgvector reuse keeps it cheap.

Voice: keep the two tiny Wyoming pods for HA. Adding speaches for an OpenAI /v1/audio surface is reasonable and cheap IF voice is actually wanted — but it is explicitly 'a plus,' so it is the lowest-priority add, not load-bearing.
Existing embeddings/rerank via llama-swap always-on group — zero new infra, correct.
Backups (Velero/CNPG/RustFS) — the actual durability story, non-negotiable.
challenges.alternative_take: "THE SIMPLEST STACK THAT HITS ALL 5 GOALS IS MOSTLY ALREADY DEPLOYED PLUS ONE CONFIG CHANGE AND ONE WORKFLOW. Concretely: (1) Local inference = llama-swap (have it). (2) Cloud passthrough = uncomment the cloud-* aliases in litellm configmap + add Anthropic key (30 min, the one real blocker). (3) Coding agent = run Claude Code or Goose as a LAPTOP CLI pointed at LiteLLM via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL — zero new in-cluster surface, top quality immediately. (4) RAG = the ONE RAG app already running (AnythingLLM) reachable as context. (5) Phone delegation = a single n8n workflow: Telegram Trigger (built-in) -> chat-ID allowlist -> spawn a K8s Job running the agent CLI in a workspace PVC -> push result back. That's it. Goals 1-5 are met with ONE config edit, ONE n8n workflow, and ONE thin agent-runner image — reusing LiteLLM, llama-swap, n8n, postgres18, the Cloudflare tunnel, and SOPS git creds. Everything else in the proposed stack (resident Goose serve, MetaMCP-as-load-bearing, mcpo, jupyter/Isola sandbox, gVisor spike, OWUI-RAG bake-off, the dedicated memory layer) is either dead cruft, infra-for-a-diagram, or premature generality. The discipline test for each proposed addition should be: 'Does a SINGLE operator delegating to THEIR OWN agent need this, today?' Sandbox isolation, MCP aggregation, cross-app shared memory, and column-store eval analytics all fail that test right now. Build them when a second user, untrusted input, or real trace volume actually materializes — not before. NOTE: session cost reached $90.28 mid-task; I stopped exploration after confirming the load-bearing facts (mem0 still wired, dead OWUI env, commented cloud aliases, MetaMCP live) rather than running further probes."

challenges.lens: Integration debt / maintenance burden on a solo-operated Talos/Flux homelab. Every self-built image, every protocol shim, and every service added to the ai/ namespace is forever-maintenance carried by one person. I evaluated each layer by asking: does this trade a one-time setup for perpetual breakage, and does it favor maintained upstream images + native integrations over hand-rolled glue?
strongest_objections: RENOVATE AUTO-MERGE IS THE SINGLE LARGEST UNADDRESSED MAINTENANCE LANDMINE, and it's worse than the proposal states. Verified in .renovate/groups.json5: the 'AI Stack' group matches mostlygeek/llama-swap and auto-merges minor/patch (autoMerge.json5: matchUpdateTypes minor/patch/pin/digest, automerge:true) with only stabilityDays:3, risk/low, ignoreTests effectively on for non-helm. There is NO allowedVersions/versioning rule constraining llama-swap to the -vulkan-b suffix. Renovate's docker datasource tracks highest semver-ish tag, so v223-rocm-bNNNN or v223-cuda-bNNNN can be auto-merged and shipped to Talos UNATTENDED, which breaks GPU inference cluster-wide with no human in the loop. The proposal correctly flags this but files it as 'hygiene' and lists it last; it is the highest-severity item in the entire stack and must land BEFORE any llama-swap bump. This is a recurring-toil generator: every llama.cpp build (daily-cadence upstream) is a candidate bump through an unconstrained auto-merge channel.

THE STACK ADDS 6-8 NEW MAINTENANCE SURFACES, AND THE MOST EXPENSIVE ONES ARE THE BUILD-YOUR-OWN IMAGES. Goose (no semver ghcr tag for Flux pin -> you pin a sha or build from in-repo Dockerfile), jupyter-kernel-gateway (NO official image exists at all -> you author and maintain a python:3.12-slim+pip Dockerfile forever, then ALSO write+maintain an MCP shim adapter in front of it because it speaks Jupyter kernel protocol not MCP), the agent-runner image (thin Dockerfile wrapping goose/claude-code+git+MCP), and the RAG-as-MCP shim (wraps AnythingLLM vector-search as an MCP server). That is up to FOUR bespoke artifacts with no upstream renovate-trackable image, each of which silently rots when its base image, its wrapped CLI, or the MCP spec drifts. On a solo homelab this is the classic 'mem0 trap' repeated four times: mem0 has been suspended for MONTHS precisely because it required a self-built image that never materialized. The proposal is recommending the same failure mode it correctly diagnosed for mem0.

THE RAG BAKE-OFF (AnythingLLM vs OWUI-RAG) IS A STANDING DUAL-MAINTENANCE TAX WITH AN UNDEFINED END DATE. Both apps run in parallel, both consume pgvector, both consume LiteLLM, and the collapse is gated on an UNVERIFIED reranker-wiring claim (OWUI RAG_RERANKING_ENGINE=external -> bge-via-llama-swap, whose exact enum string and API shape the proposal admits it did not confirm). 'Run a bake-off' on a solo homelab realistically means 'run two RAG stacks indefinitely because no one ever schedules the cutover.' The integration-honest move is to pick ONE before deploying: keep AnythingLLM (already wired, zero new shims, has a clean vector-search API for agents) OR commit to OWUI-RAG, not both. The 're-index is one-time' framing hides that the dormant-reranker and embeddings-non-portable problems make the cutover sticky enough to never happen.

AnythingLLM pg-1.13.0 IS AN OUT-OF-TREE IMAGE VARIANT BUILT BY AN UNDOCUMENTED PROCESS, which is a quiet upgrade-fragility debt the proposal under-weights. v1.13.0 source hardcodes prisma provider=sqlite; the public Dockerfile/CI do NOT emit a pg- tag; there is no official postgres install doc. Every renovate bump of the pg- line is a roll of the dice that the maintainer kept the undocumented build alive. For a solo operator this is a latent 3am-pager: a pg- tag could vanish or change schema with no migration path. This makes the 'collapse RAG into OWUI' direction MORE attractive purely on maintenance grounds (OWUI's pgvector is first-class and documented), which the proposal half-acknowledges but buries under the bake-off.

TWO-HOP ROUTING (OWUI -> owui-pipelines:9099 -> LiteLLM) IS REDUNDANT GLUE that duplicates LiteLLM's own model-group routing and creates a hidden coupling: if OWUI is ever replaced, the pipelines routing logic is silently lost unless re-encoded as LiteLLM model groups. owui-pipelines is a separate deployment + ConfigMap + reloader wiring whose entire job (code keywords->coder, short msgs->fast) could live in LiteLLM aliases the gateway already owns. This is integration debt that earns nothing today and becomes a migration trap tomorrow.

VOICE DELIBERATELY RUNS TWO PROTOCOL STACKS FOR ONE FUNCTION (Wyoming whisper+piper for HA, PLUS speaches for OpenAI /v1/audio). The proposal frames this as 'by design, not a defect' and it is defensible, but from a pure maintenance lens it is still 3 voice pods (2 Wyoming + speaches) where the agent stack today uses ZERO of them. Adding speaches is adding a release-candidate-pinned image (0.9.0-rc.3, no GA tag) to serve a north-star item the user rated 'a plus.' That is net-new forever-maintenance for a secondary feature; defer it until voice is an actual complaint.
what_to_cut: jupyter-kernel-gateway + its MCP shim (Phase-1 sandboxed code-exec): TWO bespoke maintained artifacts (self-built image with NO upstream + a hand-written MCP adapter) protecting an unsandboxed code-exec pod. This is the single worst maintenance-to-value trade in the proposal. Cut it. If an agent needs to run code, let n8n's Execute Command node or a single throwaway K8s Job handle it with a deny-egress NetworkPolicy until there is a REAL need. Isola/gVisor (Phase 2) is correctly already blocked on Talos.

speaches (for now): a release-candidate-pinned net-new pod for a 'voice is a plus' goal. The agent stack works fine text-only. Defer until a concrete voice complaint exists; the whisper.cpp-vulkan + Kokoro escalation path is the durable fallback if it ever matters.

owui-pipelines as a standing router: fold its routing into LiteLLM model groups/aliases (the gateway already does fan-out) and delete the extra deployment+ConfigMap+reloader. One fewer hop, one fewer thing to keep in sync.

Parallel RAG (one of AnythingLLM OR OWUI-RAG, not both as an open-ended bake-off): on a solo homelab, two RAG stacks is permanent dual maintenance. Keep AnythingLLM (already wired, agent-friendly vector-search API, zero new shims) UNLESS you commit a dated cutover to OWUI-RAG and actually execute it. Do not leave both running 'during the bake-off' indefinitely.
Continue.dev server, OpenHands, LibreChat, Bifrost, Windmill-as-replacement: correctly rejected by the proposal on datastore/license/build grounds. Concur fully on maintenance grounds.

Redis Agent Memory Server (kept conditional): correctly deferred. A net-new Redis-Stack pod (Dragonfly can't back RediSearch) + an LLM call per write is real perpetual cost for a capability with zero current consumer. Keep it dropped.

The agent-runner-as-K8s-Job image, IF the resident Goose pod already covers delegation: don't maintain two dispatch shapes (resident goosed serve AND a Job-spawn image). Pick one delegation mechanism.
what_to_keep: llama-swap (Vulkan) — incumbent, zero datastore, single self-contained upstream image, the ONLY engine satisfying Vulkan+hot-swap+OpenAI-compat. Lowest maintenance surface of any inference option. KEEP — but the load-bearing maintenance action is the Renovate -vulkan-b constraint + pulling llama-swap OUT of the auto-merge AI Stack group (or gating it behind a manual-review rule), done BEFORE the v179->v223 bump.

LiteLLM — incumbent, reuses postgres18+DragonflyDB, native langfuse callback, native Anthropic+OpenAI shape for the cloud-passthrough quality lever. The integration hub everything is soldered to; switching is pure churn. KEEP. (Caveat: it churns daily tags and carries 3500+ open issues — pin tightly, do not let it ride loose auto-merge either.)

Open WebUI — incumbent, single pod, native Authentik header trust, OpenAI-compat. KEEP. Mandatory cleanup: DELETE the dead MEMORY_PROVIDER/MEM0_* env (verified live at helmrelease.yaml:58-63) — it advertises an integration that cannot exist and is exactly the kind of phantom-glue that wastes a future operator's debugging time.

n8n — incumbent, postgres-only, native Telegram trigger + MCP node, existing webhook-bypass-forwardAuth routing already solved. Best-fit automation plane. KEEP — but fix the pin: it is on 2.25.5 (verified beta/prerelease channel) with no constraining renovate rule. Running unattended auto-merge on a BETA channel for the engine holding webhook+credential state is real (low-severity) operational risk. Pin to the stable 2.23.x line.

LangFuse+ClickHouse — incumbent, native LiteLLM callback (zero app code), already the most-finished integration. KEEP by default, but the proposal's measure-then-decide gate is the right maintenance posture: ClickHouse is the heaviest backing dep in ai/ and is irreversible. If span volume is low and evals unused, the Phoenix-on-postgres18 migration genuinely DELETES a stateful pod — a maintenance win worth scheduling.
MetaMCP — incumbent, postgres-only, already integrated. KEEP. Fix the stale CLAUDE.md note (lists it disabled; it is live).

Goose as the ONE agent runtime — Apache-2.0, zero datastore, no DinD, OpenAI-compat LiteLLM client. The lowest-maintenance way to add the missing agent layer. KEEP, but pin by sha/digest (no semver ghcr tag) and accept that as the cost. Use it as the SINGLE delegation mechanism — don't also build a separate Job-runner image.
Wyoming whisper+piper for HA — incumbent, tiny, native HA path. KEEP as-is (do not consolidate into speaches).

Negative-cost cleanup (do this first, it REDUCES maintenance): delete kubernetes/apps/ai/mem0/ + its ./mem0/ks.yaml line from the namespace kustomization (verified still wired), remove the dead OWUI mem0 env, fix the stale CLAUDE.md notes (MetaMCP, faster-whisper-as-Wyoming-not-OpenAI-HTTP).
challenges.alternative_take: A genuinely maintenance-minimal version of the north star collapses the in-cluster agent layer almost entirely. The proposal's own honest meta-verdict already hints at it: the cheapest Claude-Code-like experience is running Claude Code / Goose as a LAPTOP CLI pointed at the cluster's LiteLLM (cloud-backed), reserving the cluster only for async delegated jobs. Push that further for a solo operator: KEEP the deployed incumbents (llama-swap, LiteLLM, OWUI, n8n, AnythingLLM, LangFuse, MetaMCP, Wyoming) + do the negative-cost cleanup + the Renovate/n8n pin fixes, and ADD exactly ONE new thing: a single Goose pod (sha-pinned) behind Traefik+Authentik, dispatched from the already-deployed n8n via a Telegram trigger. That satisfies all five north-star goals while adding ONE renovate-untrackable artifact instead of four-to-eight. Everything else in the proposal (speaches, jupyter-kernel-gateway+MCP-shim, owui-pipelines retention, the open-ended RAG bake-off, the K8s-Job agent-runner) is net-new perpetual glue justified by speculative or secondary requirements. On a one-person homelab the dominant failure mode is not 'missing capability' — it is 'six half-maintained shims that each break quietly on a base-image bump while the operator is asleep.' The discipline that already produced single-datastore reuse should be applied just as brutally to single-NEW-artifact reuse: every build-your-own image and every protocol shim must be individually justified against the fact that the operator, not an upstream maintainer, owns it forever.

challenges.lens: Goal-fit / does-it-actually-work: will this stack deliver a Claude-Code-like autonomous coding experience, and where is the local-inference premise fundamentally limited?
strongest_objections: THE AGENT LAYER IS CLOUD-OR-NOTHING, AND THE STACK BURIES THIS. The synthesis admits 'there is no local-model substitute: the agent MUST route to Anthropic' but then keeps proposing Goose as 'primary in-cluster agent runtime' as if it were a local-inference win. It is not. A Goose/OpenHands/Aider agent driven by Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Q4 (or qwen3-14b at ~5 tok/s per the verified configmap) will NOT do useful autonomous multi-file coding. Agentic coding needs long-horizon tool-call planning, reliable structured tool output, and large effective context — exactly where 7-14B Q4 models collapse into tool-call malformation, premature stopping, and context truncation. The honest verdict is: the 'Claude-Code-like agent' is 100% a cloud-Anthropic product wearing a LiteLLM hat. Local models are relegated to autocomplete/classification. The stack's own data_flow flags this ('BLOCKER TODAY: cloud-* aliases commented OFF') but treats it as a 30-minute config fix rather than the central architectural truth: goals 2+4 do not work at all today, and only work tomorrow if you pay Anthropic.

HARDWARE REALITY IS WORSE THAN THE BRIEF CLAIMS — AND THE STACK NEVER RECONCILES IT. The brief says inference runs on the Navi 21 RX 6900 XT (16GB, 80CU) on bigboi. But the ACTUAL deployed llama-swap configmap header reads 'Single Cezanne APU iGPU (16 GiB UMA + GTT spill)' with -ngl 99 and qwen3-14b at '~5 tok/s decode'. So either (a) inference is pinned to the weak APU (8 CU, BW-bound UMA), not the dGPU, making EVERY local-model claim ~10x slower than the brief implies, or (b) the configmap is stale and contradicts the deployment. Nobody on the synthesis team caught that the comments describe the APU while the brief assumes the dGPU. At 5 tok/s, an autonomous agent doing a 20-turn tool-calling loop with 8k-context reads is unusable — a single agent task would take many minutes to tens of minutes. This is the difference between 'local agents are slow' and 'local agents are a science experiment.'

THE 'DELEGATE FROM PHONE TO IN-CLUSTER AGENT' FLOW IS THE MOST APPEALING GOAL AND THE LEAST REAL. The proposed path (Telegram -> n8n Telegram Trigger -> n8n spawns a K8s Job running Goose/Claude-Code -> result pushed back) is a chain of FOUR unbuilt integrations stacked on each other: (1) n8n must spawn a K8s Job via the API with correct RBAC, (2) an agent-runner image must exist (build-required, not deployed), (3) the runner must auth to git via SOPS and actually open a PR autonomously, (4) results must round-trip back to Telegram. The synthesis labels this 'low shim cost' because the transport pieces exist — but the transport was never the hard part. The hard part is an autonomous agent reliably completing a real coding task unattended, which is gated entirely on cloud-model quality (objection 1). This is aspirational, not a near-term deliverable.

CONTEXT-WINDOW MATH KILLS LOCAL CODING AGENTS REGARDLESS OF SPEED. The verified configmap caps qwen3-14b at --ctx-size 8192 and the coders at 16384. A Claude-Code-like agent's FIRST action is typically to read multiple files, a directory tree, and prior tool output — easily 10-40k tokens before it writes a line. An 8k-16k context local model will truncate the repo context and hallucinate file contents. This is not tunable away on 16GB VRAM: larger context = more KV cache = OOM or eviction of the model. The brief's own model lineup proves the local path cannot host an agent that needs to SEE a real codebase. The synthesis never does this arithmetic.

THE RAG/AGENT INTEGRATION IS A PILE OF UNVERIFIED SHIMS PRESENTED AS A DESIGN. Goose->RAG is 'wrap AnythingLLM vector-search as an MCP server' (build-required, ~1 day). OWUI external reranker -> bge is explicitly flagged as 'the one open question that gates this' and unverified. AnythingLLM rerank route is 'verify it targets the rerank route, not /v1 chat' (unverified). jupyter-kernel-gateway code-exec is build-your-own-image + MCP-shim + NetworkPolicy (~1-2 days). Each is plausible but NONE is proven, and the agent's usefulness depends on ALL of them composing correctly through MetaMCP's dual MCP/OpenAPI surface (itself a flagged reliability risk). The integration matrix has more 'Shim' rows in the agent path than 'Native' ones. That is the signal: the coding-agent experience is mostly glue that doesn't exist yet.
what_to_cut: Goose AS 'PRIMARY in-cluster agent-delegation runtime' — demote it. It is fine as an OSS thin client, but framing an in-cluster Goose pod driven by local Qwen as the answer to goals 2+4 is misleading. If the agent only works with cloud-Anthropic, then a LAPTOP Claude Code CLI pointed at LiteLLM (which the synthesis itself calls the 'cheapest path' in a buried meta-verdict) delivers 90% of the value with ZERO new in-cluster surface. Cut the in-cluster Goose pod from the critical path; keep it ONLY as an experiment for async cloud-backed jobs.

jupyter-kernel-gateway-behind-MCP sandboxed code-exec (Phase 1) — premature. You have no working autonomous agent yet (gated on cloud keys). Building a sandboxed code-exec MCP tool with NetworkPolicies before you have an agent that can reliably USE it is solving step 5 before step 1. Defer until a cloud-backed agent is proven to need it.

speaches voice-for-agent layer — not goal-load-bearing. The north star lists voice as 'a plus.' Adding an OpenAI /v1/audio surface for the agent stack is scope creep before the agent stack works. Keep the Wyoming pods for HA; cut speaches until there is a working agent to speak to.

The OWUI-RAG vs AnythingLLM bake-off as near-term work — defer. Two RAG stacks running in parallel pending an unverified reranker-wiring gate is effort that does not move goals 2+4 (the actual Claude-Code-like core). Pick ONE (keep AnythingLLM, it's wired) and stop; revisit only after the agent layer is real.

Telegram->n8n->K8s-Job agent-runner image build — cut from v1. This is the seductive-but-unreal flow. Replace with the boring-but-real path: laptop Claude Code CLI / Goose CLI against LiteLLM cloud aliases for interactive work; add async delegation only after that proves valuable.
what_to_keep: LiteLLM as the single gateway and cloud-passthrough chokepoint — this is the ONE component that actually makes the north star reachable, because it's where cloud-Anthropic gets wired in. Keep it, but reframe it: it is not 'limited passthrough,' it is the PRIMARY path for any real coding agent. The verified configmap confirms it's correctly built (postgres18 + DragonflyDB reuse, langfuse callback, drop_params for local backends).

llama-swap for embeddings + rerank + autocomplete-class local models — keep, because that is what local AMD inference is genuinely GOOD at. local-embed and local-rerank (always-on, ttl:0) are the real local-inference win and directly serve goal 1 (RAG). local-fast/local-coder-small for autocomplete and classification is legitimate. Just stop pretending the local chat models can drive an autonomous agent.

Open WebUI + AnythingLLM for goals 1 and 3 (RAG + phone chat) — these DO work today and DO serve real north-star items. The OWUI PWA over the Cloudflare tunnel is a genuine, deployed, working answer to 'remote access from phone.' RAG via AnythingLLM/pgvector is real. These are the parts of the stack that already deliver.

n8n as the event/automation plane for goal 2 — keep for the parts that DON'T need an autonomous coding agent (webhook -> LiteLLM inference -> notify/file-a-task, the existing linkwarden-to-anythingllm ingest pattern). This delivers a real slice of 'automate findings' without the unbuilt agent-runner.

The cleanup items (delete dead OWUI mem0 env, drop suspended mem0, fix stale CLAUDE.md voice/MetaMCP notes, harden the llama-swap Renovate rule to -vulkan- suffix) — keep ALL of these. They are pure hygiene with negative cost and the Renovate fix is genuinely load-bearing (prevents a non-functional ROCm image from auto-merging onto Talos).
challenges.alternative_take: "Stop trying to host the AGENT BRAIN locally — host only the things local AMD inference is actually good at, and treat the coding agent as an explicitly cloud-backed, mostly-laptop-resident tool. Concretely: (1) Wire LiteLLM cloud-sonnet/cloud-opus TODAY with a budget-capped, allow-listed virtual key — this is the real unlock and everything else is decoration until it's done. (2) Run Claude Code (or Goose) as a LAPTOP CLI pointed at LiteLLM's Anthropic-shaped endpoint. This delivers a genuine Claude-Code-like experience for interactive coding immediately, with zero new in-cluster surface, full LangFuse tracing, and the spend cap enforced at the gateway. It satisfies goals 2 (automate findings interactively), 4 (delegation — you delegate to the cluster's gateway), and 5 (the limited-passthrough control point) without building an agent-runner. (3) Keep the cluster's local GPU doing what it's genuinely good at: always-on embeddings/rerank for RAG (goal 1), autocomplete-class coding (local-coder-small) for cheap inline completion, and classification/routing. (4) Reserve the in-cluster async agent (Goose-in-a-Job) as a clearly-labeled v2 experiment, and ONLY build it after the laptop-cloud path proves the workflow is worth automating. The blunt reframe: the homelab's role in a Claude-Code-like stack is RAG + observability + cheap-completion + the cloud-passthrough gateway — NOT the autonomous coding brain. The local-inference dream for autonomous agentic coding is, on 16GB AMD with 8-16k context and (per the actual configmap) APU-class throughput, fundamentally limited. Pretending otherwise is where the user will be most disappointed. Also: reconcile the APU-vs-dGPU contradiction (configmap says Cezanne APU, brief says Navi 21 dGPU) before ANY local-performance claim is trusted — that single discrepancy may invalidate half the local-model latency assumptions in the synthesis."

final.executive_summary: The currently-deployed ai/ stack is ~85% correct and should mostly be KEPT: LiteLLM, llama-swap (Vulkan), Open WebUI, n8n, one RAG app, LangFuse, MetaMCP, and the Wyoming voice pods are all the right components for this Talos/AMD-Vulkan/single-datastore cluster, and the verified evidence confirms every incumbent is real, amd64, OSS, and reuses existing datastores. The biggest change is NOT adding components — it is correcting a dangerous gap and a set of dead cruft: the single highest-leverage action in the entire stack is uncommenting LiteLLM cloud-* aliases + adding an Anthropic key (a 30-minute config change that the proposal itself admits gates the ENTIRE Claude-Code-like goal), and the highest-severity risk is the unconstrained Renovate auto-merge that could ship a non-functional ROCm llama-swap image onto Talos unattended. The adversarial lenses landed three real hits I am acting on: (1) the in-cluster agent runner is cloud-or-nothing — local 7-14B Q4 models at 8-16k context (and possibly APU-class throughput, see the unreconciled configmap) cannot drive autonomous coding, so the honest answer is a LAPTOP Claude Code/Goose CLI against LiteLLM plus ONE n8n→Telegram delegation path, not a sandbox+MCP-aggregation+memory build-out; (2) the jupyter/Isola sandboxed-code-exec layer is infra-for-a-threat-model that does not exist for a solo operator and should be DROPPED; (3) LangFuse+ClickHouse is org-sized for a one-person homelab and should be measured-then-migrated to Phoenix. The go-forward stack adds exactly ONE new in-cluster artifact (a sha-pinned Goose pod) rather than the six-to-eight the maximal proposal implied.

layer_verdicts.why: Only engine satisfying Vulkan + true single-GPU hot-swap (always-on embed/rerank vs exclusive chat-swap, confirmed in configmap groups) + OpenAI-compat, with zero datastore. Bump v179-vulkan-b7565 → current vXXX-vulkan-bNNNN. LOAD-BEARING precondition: add a Renovate packageRule constraining ghcr.io/mostlygeek/llama-swap to the -vulkan-b suffix and pull it OUT of the auto-merge AI Stack group BEFORE the bump — auto-discovery tracks highest semver and can ship a v223-rocm/cuda tag that is non-functional on Talos. This is the highest-severity item in the stack, not 'hygiene'.

The single chokepoint everything is soldered to; reuses postgres18 + DragonflyDB, native langfuse callback, MIT-core for every feature in use (allow-lists, budgets, cloud passthrough), exposes BOTH OpenAI /v1 and Anthropic /v1/messages. The ONE highest-leverage action in the whole stack lives here: uncomment cloud-haiku/sonnet/opus aliases + add Anthropic key. Bump v1.87.1→stable, pin tightly (daily tags). Reject Bifrost (default SQLite = parallel state outside CNPG backup; duplicates a working layer).

Single pod, zero new datastore, native Authentik header trust (no second IdP), already the deployed PWA answering the phone-chat half of the north star. Mandatory cleanup: DELETE the dead MEMORY_PROVIDER/MEM0_* env block — confirmed no-op (upstream has no external-memory-provider concept). Reject LibreChat: drags in Mongo+Meilisearch (2-4 new stateful pods), a direct strike against single-datastore discipline.

layer_verdicts.choice: AnythingLLM (keep as the ONE RAG app)
layer_verdicts.why: REVISED from the proposal's bake-off. On a solo homelab, two parallel pgvector RAG stacks pending an UNVERIFIED reranker-wiring claim is permanent dual maintenance that never gets cut. Keep AnythingLLM (already wired, has a clean vector-search API agents can call). Do NOT start the OWUI-RAG bake-off as near-term work — it does not move the actual Claude-Code-like core (goals 2+4) and the bge-external-reranker enum/API-shape is unconfirmed. Caveat to track: the pg-1.13.0 image is an out-of-tree variant — pin tightly and verify each bump in staging. Revisit OWUI-RAG collapse only after the agent layer is real.

There is no current tool to keep, so this is the one genuine ADD — but DEMOTED from 'primary delegation runtime'. Apache-2.0, zero datastore, no DinD (Talos-clean), OpenAI-compat LiteLLM client. CRITICAL HONESTY: an in-cluster Goose driven by LOCAL Qwen will NOT do useful autonomous multi-file coding (7-14B Q4, 8-16k ctx, possibly APU throughput). It is usable ONLY routed to cloud-Anthropic via LiteLLM. Therefore deploy it as a clearly-labeled async/delegated-jobs experiment, sha/digest-pinned (no semver ghcr tag), CMD override ['goose','serve','--host','0.0.0.0','--port','3284'] (NOT 'command: server'), behind traefik-internal + Authentik. Reject OpenHands (V1 SDK has no K8s sandbox; DockerWorkspace needs docker.sock impossible on Talos; multi-user Helm paywalled). Reject Continue.dev server (build-required, Hub-coupled).

layer_verdicts.choice: Laptop Claude Code / Goose CLI → LiteLLM (cloud-backed)
layer_verdicts.why: The proposal's own buried meta-verdict and all three adversaries converge here: the cheapest path that hits goals 1-5 is a LAPTOP CLI pointed at LiteLLM via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, with zero new in-cluster surface, full LangFuse tracing, and spend cap enforced at the gateway. This is the real Claude-Code-like experience; the in-cluster pod is for async jobs only. Claude Code is proprietary (the one non-OSS sliver) — if the OSS constraint is hard, the laptop client is Goose/Aider instead. Note: from 2026-06-15 Agent SDK on subscription plans draws separate credits — use API-key auth (which LiteLLM passthrough already implies).

Postgres-only (reuses CNPG DB n8n), richest native LLM/agent surface, webhook-bypass-forwardAuth routing already solved, native Telegram trigger + MCP node. It is BOTH the goal-2 automation plane AND the goal-3+4 delegation orchestrator. FIX: it is pinned to 2.25.5, which is the BETA/prerelease channel (verified prerelease:true) with no constraining Renovate rule — pin to the stable 2.23.x line. Running unattended auto-merge on a beta channel for the engine holding webhook+credential state is a real low-severity risk.

Confirmed correct to drop. The premise (cross-app shared memory) does not hold: OWUI structurally cannot use an external provider, mem0 self-built image is permanently 404 (build-only compose), and Redis Agent Memory Server would force a NEW Redis-Stack pod (Dragonfly cannot back RediSearch) + an LLM call per write. DELETE kubernetes/apps/ai/mem0/ and its ks.yaml line. Revisit Redis Agent Memory Server via its MCP interface ONLY if a future in-cluster agent genuinely needs shared cross-run memory — as a deliberate exception, not now.

layer_verdicts.choice: MetaMCP (keep, sunk); DROP the sandbox sub-layer
layer_verdicts.why: MetaMCP is already deployed, postgres-only, sunk cost — KEEP it but do NOT treat it as load-bearing and do NOT add mcpo on top (it already exports OpenAPI). Fix the stale CLAUDE.md note (it's live, not disabled). DROP the entire jupyter-kernel-gateway-behind-MCP + Isola/gVisor sandboxed-code-exec sub-layer: a single trusted operator delegating to their OWN agent has no untrusted-code threat model; the pod boundary is the isolation story; and gVisor-on-Talos is a blocked spike (immutable rootfs, system-extension required). This is the single largest piece of unearned infra in the proposal.

layer_verdicts.choice: llama.cpp embed/rerank via llama-swap always-on group
layer_verdicts.why: The ONLY option that runs on AMD-Vulkan (TEI/infinity are CUDA/ROCm-Instinct-only), zero new infra, models already resident and OpenAI-compat behind LiteLLM. This is what local AMD inference is genuinely GOOD at and directly serves goal 1 (RAG). Optional maturity-phase A/B: embed-nomic → Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-GGUF for better recall, same host. Verify AnythingLLM rerank config targets LiteLLM's rerank route (Jina/Cohere shape), not the /v1 chat path.

KEEP the two tiny Wyoming pods for Home Assistant Assist — they work and are the path of least resistance. DEFER speaches: voice is explicitly 'a plus' in the north star, and adding a release-candidate-pinned net-new pod (no GA tag) for the agent /v1/audio surface before the agent stack even works is scope creep. The agent stack functions text-only. Add speaches (or the whisper.cpp-vulkan + Kokoro split) only when voice is an actual complaint. Note: the CLAUDE.md claim that faster-whisper is an OpenAI-HTTP server is factually wrong — it's Wyoming-only; fix the doc.

layer_verdicts.choice: LangFuse + ClickHouse (KEEP, with measured trip-wire to Phoenix)
layer_verdicts.why: Native LiteLLM langfuse callback (zero app code) + already-configured Authentik OIDC make it the most-finished integration; ClickHouse is sunk cost. BUT all three adversaries agree it is org-sized for one operator and ClickHouse is the heaviest, irreversible backing dep with LangFuse as its sole consumer. Action: measure ClickHouse span volume + whether evals are used; if low/unused, schedule the Arize Phoenix (postgres18-only, external-DB mode, OTLP via LiteLLM callbacks:['otel']) migration to DELETE the ClickHouse pod. Default KEEP until measured — do not pay a migration to fix an unproven problem.

OWUI PWA over the Cloudflare tunnel already solves mobile chat — KEEP unchanged. ADD a single n8n workflow: Telegram Trigger (built-in core node) → chat-ID allowlist → dispatch to the cloud-backed agent (laptop CLI result relay, or the v2 in-cluster Goose pod) → push result back. Reuses already-deployed n8n+LiteLLM+tunnel, opens NO new public port (Telegram egresses out — a security WIN). This is the genuinely high-value add. Reject LibreChat/Matrix-Synapse (new stateful infra).

layer_verdicts.choice: Fold routing into LiteLLM model groups
layer_verdicts.why: Redundant routing hop the proposal's own conflicts section names: OWUI→pipelines:9099→LiteLLM is two planes doing keyword→model fan-out. LiteLLM already owns aliasing/fallback/routing. Re-encode the pipelines logic (code-keywords→coder, short→fast) as LiteLLM model groups and delete the extra Deployment+ConfigMap+reloader. One fewer hop, one fewer thing to keep in sync, and it removes a silent migration trap if OWUI is ever replaced. Low priority but correct.

final.recommended_stack:
TARGET STACK (one new in-cluster artifact, several deletions, two config unlocks):

CLIENTS (mostly off-cluster)
  - Laptop Claude Code / Goose CLI  ──ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL──┐   (PRIMARY interactive coding agent, cloud-backed)
  - Open WebUI PWA (ai.68cc.io, phone)  ──OPENAI_API_BASE_URL──┐
  - n8n editor / webhooks ──┐                                   │
  - AnythingLLM (RAG UI/API) ──┐                                │
  - Telegram bot → n8n Telegram Trigger → dispatch ──┐          │
                                                      ▼          ▼
GATEWAY (the single chokepoint)
  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ LiteLLM :4000  (OpenAI /v1 + Anthropic /v1/messages)               │
  │   • reuses CNPG postgres18 (DB litellm) + DragonflyDB DB4          │
  │   • virtual keys + model allow-lists (kid-safe lever)             │
  │   • success/failure_callback:[langfuse]                           │
  │   • cloud-* aliases UNCOMMENTED + Anthropic key  ◄── THE UNLOCK   │
  └───────────────┬───────────────────────────┬──────────────────────┘
                  │ local aliases             │ cloud passthrough (budget-capped)
                  ▼                           ▼
  llama-swap :8080 (Vulkan)            Anthropic / OpenAI
   • chat group: exclusive hot-swap     (the only path that makes an
     qwen3-4b/14b, qwen-coder 3b/7b      autonomous coding agent usable)
   • always-on: embed-nomic + bge-rerank (RESIDENT — the real local win)
   • GGUFs on openebs PVC, init-fetch
   • [RECONCILE: APU vs Navi 21 dGPU — configmap says Cezanne APU]

AGENT (v2 / async only)
  + Goose pod (sha-pinned, goose serve, traefik-internal + Authentik)
    → cloud-backed via LiteLLM only; dispatched from n8n for delegated jobs

SUPPORTING (all KEEP, reuse existing datastores)
  - MetaMCP (postgres18) — MCP aggregation, sunk, not load-bearing
  - LangFuse + ClickHouse — observability (measure → maybe Phoenix)
  - Wyoming whisper + piper — Home Assistant Assist voice ONLY
  - Velero / CNPG Barman / RustFS S3 — durability (non-negotiable)

INGRESS: Traefik Gateway API → Authentik forwardAuth (.17 LAN / .15 public via CF tunnel)

DELETED: mem0 dir+ks.yaml · OWUI mem0 env · owui-pipelines (folded into LiteLLM)
NOT BUILT: jupyter/Isola sandbox · gVisor spike · mcpo · OWUI-RAG bake-off (now) · speaches (now) · dedicated memory layer · K8s-Job agent-runner (Goose serve covers it)
changes_vs_current: UNLOCK (highest leverage): uncomment cloud-haiku/sonnet/opus aliases in litellm configmap.yaml and add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (+ optional OPENAI_API_KEY) to litellm-secrets via SOPS. Today they are commented OFF and no key is set — the ENTIRE Claude-Code-like goal (goals 2+4) is blocked by a comment character, not by missing components.

SAFETY (highest severity): add a Renovate packageRule pinning ghcr.io/mostlygeek/llama-swap to the -vulkan-b suffix AND pull it out of the auto-merge AI Stack group (or gate behind manual review) BEFORE bumping v179→current. Without it, auto-discovery can merge a v223-rocm/cuda tag that is non-functional on Talos, breaking GPU inference cluster-wide unattended.

RECONCILE (blocks trust in all local-perf claims): the deployed configmap header says 'Single Cezanne APU iGPU' and helmrelease sets GGML_VK_VISIBLE_DEVICES:0 with no visible bigboi nodeAffinity, but the brief assumes the Navi 21 RX 6900 XT. Confirm which GPU actually runs inference. qwen3-14b is annotated ~5 tok/s — if that's the APU, local autonomous agents are a science experiment, not slow-but-usable.

ADD ONE artifact: a sha/digest-pinned Goose pod (ghcr.io/aaif-goose/goose), CMD ['goose','serve','--host','0.0.0.0','--port','3284'], behind traefik-internal + Authentik — labeled as an async/delegated-jobs experiment, cloud-backed only.
ADD ONE workflow: Telegram bot → n8n Telegram Trigger → chat-ID allowlist → dispatch to cloud-backed agent → result push-back. Opens no new public port.
DELETE mem0: remove kubernetes/apps/ai/mem0/ and its '- ./mem0/ks.yaml' line from ai/kustomization.yaml. Image is permanently 404.
DELETE dead OWUI env: strip MEMORY_PROVIDER/MEM0_API_BASE_URL/MEM0_API_KEY from open-webui helmrelease.yaml — confirmed no-op faking an integration that cannot exist.
FOLD owui-pipelines routing into LiteLLM model groups, then delete the pipelines Deployment+ConfigMap+reloader.
PIN n8n to the stable 2.23.x line (currently on 2.25.5 beta/prerelease) and add a Renovate rule constraining it off the prerelease channel.
FIX stale docs in CLAUDE.md: MetaMCP is live (not disabled); faster-whisper is Wyoming-only (not an OpenAI-HTTP server); reconcile the APU/dGPU narrative.

DO NOT BUILD (vs the maximal proposal): jupyter-kernel-gateway+MCP-shim sandbox, Isola/gVisor, mcpo, the open-ended OWUI-RAG bake-off, speaches, a dedicated memory service, and a separate K8s-Job agent-runner image. Each fails the 'does a single operator delegating to their OWN agent need this today' test.
phased_rollout: PHASE 0 — Negative-cost cleanup + safety (do FIRST, reduces risk and surface): add the llama-swap -vulkan-b Renovate rule and remove it from auto-merge; pin n8n to 2.23.x; delete mem0 dir+ks.yaml; strip dead OWUI mem0 env; fix stale CLAUDE.md notes. No new capability, pure de-risking. ~1-2h.

PHASE 1 — The real unlock (lowest risk, highest value): uncomment LiteLLM cloud-* aliases + add Anthropic key (SOPS). Verify budget-capped allow-listed virtual key works. Then run Claude Code / Goose as a LAPTOP CLI against LiteLLM's Anthropic endpoint. This alone delivers a genuine interactive Claude-Code-like experience for goals 2,4,5 with ZERO new in-cluster surface. MEASURE whether the laptop-cloud path satisfies the workflow before building anything in-cluster. ~1h config + real use.

PHASE 2 — Reconcile hardware + bump engine: confirm APU-vs-dGPU reality; if inference can be moved to / is on the Navi 21 dGPU, validate throughput. Bump llama-swap to the current vXXX-vulkan-bNNNN (now safe behind the Phase-0 Renovate rule). Confirm always-on embed/rerank stay resident. ~half day.

PHASE 3 — Delegation path (only if Phase 1 proved the workflow worth automating): build the n8n Telegram→allowlist→dispatch→push-back workflow. Initially relay to the laptop CLI or a one-shot run; defer the resident Goose pod. ~1 day.

PHASE 4 — In-cluster async agent (optional, v2 experiment): deploy the sha-pinned Goose serve pod behind traefik-internal+Authentik, cloud-backed via LiteLLM, dispatched from the Phase-3 n8n workflow. Smoke-test the no-Docker-socket in-pod tool isolation. Accept it is cloud-quality only. ~half day.

PHASE 5 — Observability right-sizing (independent, schedule when convenient): measure ClickHouse span volume + eval usage; if low/unused, migrate LangFuse→Arize Phoenix on postgres18 (LiteLLM callbacks:['otel']) and delete the ClickHouse pod. Epic-worthy, one-directional — only if measurement justifies it.

DEFERRED INDEFINITELY (build only on concrete trigger): speaches/voice-for-agent (trigger: a voice complaint); OWUI-RAG collapse (trigger: agent layer real + bge wiring empirically verified); sandboxed code-exec (trigger: untrusted callers / kids actually use it); Redis Agent Memory Server (trigger: an agent genuinely needs shared cross-run memory).
honest_limitations: LOCAL AUTONOMOUS CODING IS NOT VIABLE ON THIS HARDWARE. 7-14B Q4 models with 8-16k context (qwen3-14b at --ctx-size 8192) cannot do reliable long-horizon, multi-file, tool-calling coding — they malform tool calls, stop prematurely, and truncate repo context. A real agent's FIRST action (read several files + a tree) easily exceeds the context budget, and more context = more KV cache = OOM/eviction on 16GB. The 'Claude-Code-like agent' is, honestly, a cloud-Anthropic product wearing a LiteLLM hat. Local models are relegated to embeddings, rerank, autocomplete, and classification — which is exactly what they're good at.

THROUGHPUT MAY BE FAR WORSE THAN THE BRIEF IMPLIES. The deployed configmap describes a Cezanne APU (8 CU, BW-bound UMA) at ~5 tok/s for the 14B, while the brief assumes the Navi 21 dGPU (80 CU). Until that contradiction is reconciled, every local-model latency assumption is suspect. At APU throughput, even a cloud-fallback agent's local-model steps (classification/routing) are slow, and a 20-turn local loop is unusable.

THE PHONE→IN-CLUSTER-AGENT DELEGATION FLOW IS THE MOST APPEALING GOAL AND THE LEAST PROVEN. The transport (Telegram→n8n) is real and cheap, but an autonomous agent reliably completing a real coding task UNATTENDED and opening a correct PR is gated entirely on cloud-model quality — the transport was never the hard part. Expect this to work well only cloud-backed, and to need iteration on the agent's git/PR loop.

GOOSE IN-CLUSTER ADDS A RENOVATE-UNTRACKABLE ARTIFACT. No semver ghcr tag means pinning a sha/digest forever; you own the bump cadence manually. Acceptable as the single new artifact, but it is genuine perpetual toil on a solo homelab.

MetaMCP is an aggregation layer with almost nothing to aggregate (one real consumer + a github MCP). It's sunk cost so keep it, but it is premature generality for one operator — do not let it become load-bearing.

Claude Code (the best interactive option) is PROPRIETARY — the one non-OSS sliver in the north star. The OSS-only constraint is satisfied only if you accept Goose/Aider as the laptop client instead, at some quality cost. The cloud passthrough also means the 'fully OSS, fully local' ideal is not achievable for usable agentic coding on this hardware — that ideal is fundamentally limited here.

The homelab's real role in a Claude-Code-like stack is RAG + observability + cheap-completion + the cloud-passthrough gateway — NOT the autonomous coding brain. Setting any other expectation is where you will be most disappointed.
open_decisions: GPU TRUTH: Is local inference actually running on the Navi 21 RX 6900 XT (bigboi) or the Cezanne APU (bee-jms-03)? The deployed configmap says APU; the brief says dGPU. This needs your confirmation — it determines whether local models are 'slow but usable' or 'science experiment', and whether moving the workload to the dGPU is worth the scheduling work.

OSS PURITY vs QUALITY: For the interactive coding agent, do you accept proprietary Claude Code (best quality, needs Anthropic key/subscription) as the laptop client, or must it be OSS (Goose/Aider, lower agentic quality)? This is a hard either/or given local models can't fill the gap.

CLOUD SPEND TOLERANCE: What monthly budget cap do you want on the cloud-* virtual key(s)? The agent only works cloud-backed, so this is the real recurring cost. Also: from 2026-06-15 Claude Agent SDK on subscription plans draws separate credits — confirm you'll use API-key auth via LiteLLM (recommended) to avoid subscription-cap surprises.

LANGFUSE/CLICKHOUSE: Do you actually use evals + high trace volume, or just the trace viewer + cost dashboard? If the latter, do you want to schedule the Phoenix migration to delete the ClickHouse pod? (Needs a one-time measurement of ClickHouse span volume first.)
DELEGATION TRANSPORT: Telegram bot (cheapest, no new public port, but a third-party SaaS) vs an OSS-pure path (Matrix/Synapse = new stateful infra). Telegram is recommended; confirm it's acceptable.

RAG DIRECTION: Confirm you're OK keeping AnythingLLM as the single RAG app and NOT running the OWUI-RAG bake-off now. If you specifically want the bge cross-encoder reranker active for RAG (it's dormant under AnythingLLM/pgvector), that's the one reason to revisit OWUI-RAG — but only after empirically verifying the external-reranker wiring.
